title: Energy market on edge amid Middle East tensions
section_path: Energy market on edge amid Middle East tensions
section_path_text: Energy market on edge amid Middle East tensions
summary:
Main
Tensions in the Middle East remained elevated, keeping energy markets on edge. A stronger USD weighed in investor appetite in the base and precious metal markets. By Daniel Hynes
Image reference: Στιγμιότυπο οθόνης 2025-06-19 150427.png | assets/2025-06-19_energy-market-on-edge-amid-middle-east-tensions_img_cea3cf84ceb9ceb3cebcceb9cf8ccf84cf85_757e5a8e23da.png

Market Commentary
Crude oil prices edged higher, briefly touching a year-to-date high amid speculation that the US will become involved in the Israel-Iran conflict.

children.title: Main
children.summary: Tensions in the Middle East remained elevated, keeping energy markets on edge. A stronger USD weighed in investor appetite in the base and precious metal markets.

children.title: Market Commentary
children.summary: Crude oil prices edged higher, briefly touching a year-to-date high amid speculation that the US will become involved in the Israel-Iran conflict. Trump said Iran had squandered the chance to make a deal over its nuclear enrichment but declined to say whether the US plans to join Israel's offensive aimed at destroying the program.

children.title: Chart of the Day
children.summary: China's copper inventories have been continuing to fall despite the uncertain economic outlook. On a seasonal basis, warehouse stockpiles are near their lowest seen over the past 15 years, highlighting the tightness in the market.
